# Notion Template Guide

These templates are meant to be copied into a local `notion/` directory next to each lab or capstone.

## Quality bar

A filled notebook should be usable after the implementation details have been forgotten. That means:

- complete technical sentences
- explicit uncertainty instead of invented certainty
- concrete commands, test names, or log evidence
- short explanation of why a design choice was made

## Minimum local file set

- `00-problem-framing.md`
- `01-approach-log.md`
- `02-debug-log.md`
- `03-retrospective.md`
- `04-knowledge-index.md`

## Rule of thumb

If a page reads like a template after you fill it, it is still too shallow. Replace prompt text with specific evidence from the lab you worked on.
